results.title: Fed interest rate decision April 2026: Fed holds rates steady amid dissent

Federal Reserve Board - Federal Reserve issues FOMC statement
Federal Reserve leaves interest rates unchanged as Powell's chairmanship nears end

What Does the Fed's April Rate Decision Signal for 2026?
Fed meeting recap: Powell to stay on board – Trump's legal attacks have 'left me no choice'

summary_raw: April 29, 2026: FOMC held rates at 3.5%-3.75% in an unusual 8-4 split vote (highest dissent since Oct 1992). Third consecutive hold after three consecutive cuts in 2025. Inflation cited as elevated partly due to energy prices. Middle East uncertainty driving caution. Powell staying on Fed board despite end of chairmanship. One Trump appointee (Miran) dissented for immediate cut; three others dissented against future-cut language.
